depthCommitment.interpretation: These are reviewed planning floors, not authored steps or pedagogy proof. Every future interaction must have one observable purpose and pass content, behavior, accessibility, duplication, and learner-review gates.

assessmentBoundary.externalExamEvidence: The pinned external exam exposes no reviewable item bank and receives zero concept credit.
requiredOriginalEvidence: A published competency and cognitive-demand blueprint.
Original secure item and performance-task banks with parallel forms.
Behavior, changed-case, diagnosis, accessibility, design-defense, correction, retention, and transfer evidence.
Accessibility, content-validity, bias, form-balance, scoring, standard-setting, pilot, and item-analysis review.

architectureFindings: Every candidate module now has explicit model, guided workshop, faded build, debugging clinic, independent lab, retrieval review, and assessment planning records.
Scenario titles and domains are unique across the matrix; uniqueness does not prove semantic originality, so review remains blocking.
Module activity records apply their evidence stages to every named new concept, while retrieval reviews also name earlier concepts from the repaired architecture.
Interaction floors exceed the pinned depth inventory but cannot be reported as authored until original learner-facing steps and checks exist.
gaps: Concept clusters inside each planned activity still need authored step-level objectives, examples, checks, hints, feedback, correction branches, and delayed scheduling.
Independent subject, instructional-design, assessment, accessibility, runtime, duplication, and representative-learner reviews remain open.
The original certification blueprint, item banks, scoring evidence, and standard-setting work remain unauthored and unvalidated.
No planned interaction may become published merely because this matrix is schema-valid or exceeds a count floor.
